description: You are a remote sensing technician and spatial analyst working on soil contamination assessment for a flood plain monitoring project using the Meuse river dataset. Conduct a geostatistical analysis of log-transformed zinc concentrations using the gstat and sp packages in /home/ga/RProjects/spatial_analysis.R. The analysis must include a fitted variogram model (spherical or exponential), ordinary kriging on a 40m x 40m grid, and a Moran's I test for residual spatial autocorrelation. Export the variogram parameters to /home/ga/RProjects/output/zinc_variogram.csv (columns: model, nugget, sill, range_m) and empirical points to /home/ga/RProjects/output/zinc_variogram_points.csv (columns: dist, gamma, np). Save back-transformed kriging predictions (exp(prediction)) and variance to /home/ga/RProjects/output/zinc_kriging_predictions.csv (columns: x, y, zinc_pred, zinc_var). Record Moran's I results in /home/ga/RProjects/output/zinc_moran_test.csv (columns: statistic, expected, variance, p_value, significant). Generate a 2-panel spatial figure showing a bubble map of observed concentrations and a kriging prediction map to /home/ga/RProjects/output/zinc_kriging_map.png. Login: username=ga, password=password123.

metadata.dataset: Meuse river soil zinc concentrations (sp package built-in dataset, 155 samples)
metadata.task_note: Uses sp::meuse built-in data; gstat + sp + ape packages needed
